heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Luchino Visconti. Written by Suso Cecchi D'Amico, Pasquale Festa Campanile, Enrico Medioli, Massimo Franciosa, and Luchino Visconti, based on the 1958 novel The Leopard by Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa.
clean: Directed by Luchino Visconti. Written by Suso Cecchi D'Amico, Pasquale Festa Campanile, Enrico Medioli, Massimo Franciosa, and Luchino Visconti, based on the 1958 novel The Leopard by Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa.

heading: Don Fabrizio Corbera
raw: We were the leopards, the lions, those who take our place will be jackals and sheep, and the whole lot of us - leopards, lions, jackals and sheep - will continue to think ourselves the salt of the earth.
clean: We were the leopards, the lions, those who take our place will be jackals and sheep, and the whole lot of us - leopards, lions, jackals and sheep - will continue to think ourselves the salt of the earth.

heading: Don Fabrizio Corbera
raw: [of the Sicilians] They never want to improve. They think themselves perfect. Their vanity is greater than their misery.
clean: They never want to improve. They think themselves perfect. Their vanity is greater than their misery.

heading: Don Fabrizio Corbera
raw: You know what is happening in our country? Nothing... simply an imperceptible replacement of one class for another. The middle class doesn't want to destroy us. It simply wants to take our place... and very gently.
clean: You know what is happening in our country? Nothing... simply an imperceptible replacement of one class for another. The middle class doesn't want to destroy us. It simply wants to take our place... and very gently.

heading: Don Fabrizio Corbera
raw: [to Father Pirrone] You're used to naked souls. You should know that naked bodies are far more innocent.
clean: You're used to naked souls. You should know that naked bodies are far more innocent.

heading: Don Fabrizio Corbera
raw: Things will have to change in order that they remain the same.
clean: Things will have to change in order that they remain the same.

heading: Don Fabrizio Corbera
raw: Yes, love, of course! Fire and flames for a year, ashes for thirty. I too know what love is.
clean: Yes, love, of course! Fire and flames for a year, ashes for thirty. I too know what love is.

heading: Don Fabrizio Corbera
raw: We're not blind in spirit, Father. We're just human beings in a changing world.
clean: We're not blind in spirit, Father. We're just human beings in a changing world.

heading: Tancredi Falconeri
raw: If we want things to stay as they are, things will have to change.
clean: If we want things to stay as they are, things will have to change.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Don Fabrizio Corbera: And you, Don Ciccio, how did you vote on the 21st?
clean: And you, Don Ciccio, how did you vote on the 21st?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Don Fabrizio Corbera: What are you afraid of? There's no one else here but us, the wind, the dogs.
clean: What are you afraid of? There's no one else here but us, the wind, the dogs.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Don Francisco Ciccio Tumeo: Excuse me, Excellency, but that question is useless. You know that everyone in Donnafugata voted, voted 'yes'. They said you, yourself, advised those who couldn't decide to vote like that.
clean: Excuse me, Excellency, but that question is useless. You know that everyone in Donnafugata voted, voted 'yes'. They said you, yourself, advised those who couldn't decide to vote like that.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Don Fabrizio Corbera: I did, indeed. So, you voted 'yes', hmm...
clean: I did, indeed. So, you voted 'yes', hmm...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Father Pirrone: Is Your Excellency here for confession?
clean: Is Your Excellency here for confession?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Don Fabrizio Corbera: Confession? It's not Saturday.
clean: Confession? It's not Saturday.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Father Pirrone: Listen to me. Come to confession.
clean: Listen to me. Come to confession.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Don Fabrizio Corbera: There's no need. You know it all already.
clean: There's no need. You know it all already.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Father Pirrone: The power of confession lies not only in telling our sins, but in repentance.
clean: The power of confession lies not only in telling our sins, but in repentance.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Angelica Sedara: Listen to me. Concetta is right. To marry him after loving you would be like drinking water after tasting... [They kiss]
clean: Listen to me. Concetta is right. To marry him after loving you would be like drinking water after tasting...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tancredi Falconeri: Tasting what?
clean: Tasting what?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Angelica Sedara: Marsala. [They kiss again]
clean: Marsala.
